summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their organizational and regular meeting for 2026, electing Trustee Hauserman as Chairperson and Trustee Hodges as Vice Chairperson. The State of the Township address highlighted public safety achievements, $1.3 million growth in unencumbered funds, and ongoing infrastructure projects including Station 18 construction. A significant zoning update revealed that a 2005 rezoning of the Kazaddale Road property was never legally approved, halting a pending development. The board also addressed organizational matters, approved zoning fee schedules, discussed a proposed Station 18 parking lot expansion, and accepted two firefighter resignations.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda passed unanimously
Motion to approve payment of bills (December 10 through January 27) passed unanimously
Trustee Hauserman nominated and elected as 2026 Board Chairperson (2-0, Hauserman abstained)
Trustee Hodges nominated and elected as 2026 Vice Chairperson (2-0, Hodges abstained)
Motion to adopt 2026 Board of Trustees regular meeting schedule passed unanimously
Motion to set 2026 standard mileage rate at 72.5 cents per business mile passed unanimously
Resolution R001-2026 authorizing sale of surplus items on GovDeals.com passed unanimously
Resolution R002-2026 authorizing department heads to communicate directly with Clermont County Prosecutor's Office passed unanimously

Motion to schedule Board of Trustees special meeting/zoning workshop for February 17 at 6 PM passed unanimously
Direction given to Zoning Director to prepare resolution to rezone State Route 28 Land Bank parcels from M-2 to B-1

Motion to adjourn into executive session passed unanimously
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Emily Zisi effective January 22, 2026 passed unanimously
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Aaron Kidd effective January 21, 2026 passed unanimously
Motion to adjourn at 7:38 PM passed unanimously

attendees: Administrator Bob Rose (also referenced as Chief Rose, Police Chief)
Chief Myers (Fire/EMS Chief, also referenced as Steve)
Mr. Hunter (new resident, former Stonelick Township trustee)
Evan Cordray (added as authorized credit card user)
Tasha Bailey (Goshen Chamber of Commerce president, not present)
notes: This was a combined organizational and regular meeting for the start of 2026. The Kazaddale Road zoning finding is significant - a 2005 vote that was assumed to have rezoned the property to PUD was determined to be legally invalid because only one trustee voted affirmative (one abstained, one absent), failing to meet the required majority. The property reverts to R-1 zoning and is subject to the current moratorium through April 22, 2026. The meeting date in the video title shows 1-27-26, confirming January 27, 2026. A new resident, Mr. Hunter, was recognized as a former 20-year Stonelick Township trustee. The board went into executive session regarding employment/discipline of a public employee but no action was reported from the session. The Fourth of July parade discussion was notable as the motion was allowed to 'die' rather than being formally tabled, with the board wanting further conversation with the Chamber and safety services about staffing feasibility.
title: BOT 1-27-26
